economic_notes: Widjikiwe Gas Bar L.P. operates as a government business enterprise (not consolidated due to data unavailability). TFN Construction L.P. is consolidated. Widjikiwe Holdings Corp. owns 51% of Wawatay-RJL Drilling Inc. since November 2023. First Nation signed a contract generating income for 7 years with future minimum income of $813,377. WGB has exclusive supply contracts for gasoline (expires Nov 2029) and general merchandise (expires Nov 2027).
notes: Audit is QUALIFIED for two reasons: (1) failure to identify and measure asset retirement obligations under PS 3280, and (2) Widjikiwe Gas Bar L.P. not accounted for using the equity method due to inability to obtain data. Consolidated entities include Widjikiwe Holdings Corp., Widjikiwe L.P., TFN Construction L.P.; WGB excluded. Wawatay-RJL Drilling Inc. owned 51% by WHC since Nov 2023 with no transactions in 2023-24. ISC funding calculated as sum of Fixed, Block, Flexible and Set Contributions ($6,270,737 + $16,600,313 + $1,182,669 + $485,447). Total government transfers estimated to include ISC, CMHC, FNEC, SRPNI, Securite publique du Quebec, Public Safety Canada, FNQLHSSC, Health Canada, Ministere de la Culture, ANHRSDC, Western Quebec School Board and other government sources. Segment disclosure (Schedule 3) is partially cut off. No schedule of remuneration provided. Community Hall/Youth Center Construction reserve of $3,578,682 was returned to cumulative operating surplus in 2024. Current liabilities include bank loan $65,000 + accounts payable $4,595,030 + ISC advance $2,718,689 + Due to ISC $711,595 + Due to Widjikiwe Gas Bar $187,043 + current portion of long-term debt $705,133 = $8,982,490 but ISC advance appears to be 2023 item not in 2024 balance; recalculated current liabilities as $4,595,030 + $711,595 + $187,043 + $705,133 + $65,000 = $6,263,801 plus deferred revenue shown separately.
